plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-541 RP17.P07.M04.S06-RP17.P07.M05.S20 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-541 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP17.P07 workflow permission slice: the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice tail (the twenty remaining rows) and the M05 Permission And Audit Binding head (the first twenty rows) as two descriptor-only sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H17), Claude review packet (C17), and CP00-542 handoff, without opening AI governance, model policy, retrieval scope, or citation runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-541 is a descriptor-only Risk B pack (RP17.P07 workflow permission slice, 40 units, RP17.P07.M04.S06-RP17.P07.M05.S20) that correctly claims production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, no_real_data=true, writes_product_state=false (manifest.json:6-9; command-evidence.json:6-8), with the pack-level claim limited to the descriptor-verified flag and all runtime opening deferred to CP00-542 under required human approval. The claim is fully supported by independent inspection: the CP541 pack binding (registry.js:460-475), no-write attestation (registry.js:2322-2326), and requirements (registry.js:2328-2373) are consistent with the case-set/descriptor builders (service.js:3461-3532) and the coverage/slice/Hermes/Claude/handoff validators (validators.js:4951-5096); freezeCp541Result stamps a comprehensive no-write/no-runtime/no-leak attestation set (service.js:3413-3455) and every row is forced descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false (validators.js:5012-5016). Deliverable distribution {failure_recovery:19, security_audit:4, implementation:6, hermes_evidence:4, fixture:2, test:4, claude_review:1} sums to 40 and matches the prompt baseline, registry requirements (registry.js:2343-2351), and the live closeout-pack-plan entry (closeout-pack-plan.json:407-418); micro-title distribution {Secondary Workflow Slice:20, Permission And Audit Binding:20} matches the baseline included_units. All 7 required exports exist, are registered (registry.js:2362-2368), and are surfaced publicly via index.js wildcard re-exports (index.js:1-3); the README CP00-541 section (README.md:113-115) accurately describes the slice. Boundaries are solid: no fs/network/child_process/IO anywhere in packages/ai-governance/src, the validator, or the test; the contract projection mirrors the generated descriptor (ai-governance-core-contract.json:26750-26767) and the validator deep-equals it plus cp541_requirements/cp541_no_write_attestation against the registry constants (validate-rp17...mjs:1490-1496), with all command-evidence commands (npm test, npm run validate, rp17/rp00/plan/weighted/spec/fullplan/goal validates, git diff --check) recorded exit 0. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not final approver (read_only:true, tools Read/Grep/Glob only, forbidden_tools_present:false; claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false), and human final approval for any enterprise trust claim remains required. Hermes control-plane patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor receipt handling (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, no_hermes_runtime_receipt:true, receipt_shape descriptor_only_governance_p07_workflow_permission_slice_no_write); hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false holds. CP178+ review hardening is encoded (forbidden_review_evidence includes failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, missing fields) and the readiness auth-preflight passed with non-zero stdout and valid JSON (readiness-report.json:8-16). Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly still pending at this review step. One non-blocking P3: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies two untracked files in both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (the same cosmetic tooling inconsistency flagged in CP00-540). No P0/P1/P2 issues; does not block pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies two untracked files as both pack-implementation and unrelated-preserved
findings.details: The generated scope artifact lists docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-541/ in untracked_implementation_files (lines 152-153) and ALSO in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 158-159). These two classifications are mutually exclusive in intent. The pack manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest.json:1719-1730) excludes both of these (it correctly treats the pack's own docs/closeout-packs/cp00-541/ directory as pack-related), so the two generated artifacts disagree on the categorization. active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty (line 155) and active_pack_required_untracked_files is empty (line 15), so no required file is dropped and the effective pack scope (8 active implementation refs) is unaffected. This is a cosmetic inconsistency in review tooling metadata, not in any pack deliverable, and has no functional impact on the descriptor-only boundaries or the production_ready=false claim. This is the same issue raised as CP00-540-P3-01, indicating the scope generator was not corrected between packs.
findings.recommendation: Align the scope generator so a file appears in only one of untracked_implementation_files or unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, matching the manifest's worktree_transition_policy categorization (treat docs/closeout-packs/cp00-541/ as pack-related). No change required to pack source, tests, contract, or boundaries.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking informational. Does not affect pack scope determination, descriptor-only boundaries, tests, validation, or closeout gating; recommend the Codex side correct the generator output in a future tooling pass.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-541 ai governance core p07 workflow permission slice descriptor pack
